news.title: All The Celebrities Spotted At 2026 FIFA World Cup Final Match
news.lede: The 2026 FIFA World Cup Final, pitting Argentina against Spain at MetLife Stadium, concluded as an unprecedented fusion of sport and entertainment. Marking a historic first, the event featured a star-studded Halftime Show, curated by Coldplay's Chris Martin, with performances from Madonna, Shakira, Justin Bieber, and BTS, benefiting the FIFA Global Citizen Education Fund. The closing ceremony also showcased musical acts including Jennifer Hudson, Post Malone, and Robbie Williams. Beyond the stage, the stands were a who's who of global figures, from David Beckham and Jay-Z to Tom Cruise and Kylie Jenner.

news.title: Fox Sports compares World Cup France to Ohio State in college football
news.lede: While soccer -- ahem, football -- is the world's game, American football is the game of the United States, by far. The NFL is the most-watched professional sports league in this country, and right behind that on the amateur front (less and less every day) is college football. Across the rest of the world, soccer is far and above the sport everyone is passionate about. So, then, with this year's World Cup taking place in North America, including the United States, Fox Sports married the two together and decided to find four college football teams that best compare to the four semifinal teams of the 2026 World Cup.

news.title: Football: Spain beat holders Argentina in extra time to lift 2nd World Cup
news.lede: NEW YORK - Spain lifted the World Cup for the second time Sunday after beating 10-man defending champions Argentina 1-0 in extra time. Substitute Ferran Torres scored the 106th-minute winner for a dominant Spain at New York/New Jersey Stadium, where Argentina managed two shots, neither on target, and had Enzo Fernandez sent off for a second yellow deep in the second half. President Donald Trump was present for the game and attended the award ceremony after the match, which was interrupted by an unusual 27-minute halftime show. Captain Rodri was named the tournament's best player as the European champions, managed by Luis de la Fuente, enjoyed a near-perfect campaign that began with a 0-0 draw with debutants Cape Verde but ended with the trophy.

news.title: 2026 World Cup results, standings and schedule: Live scores, knockout round updates and how to watch
news.lede: The 2026 FIFA World Cup final is set with reigning champions Argentina looking for back-to-back titles as they go against 2010 winners Spain. The three tournament co-hosts were each knocked out in the Round of 16 after Canada lost Morocco, Mexico fell to England and the USMNT was eliminated by Belgium. Below is a complete look at every group's final standings, which teams advanced, which were eliminated and the full knockout round schedule. Thursday, June 11: Mexico 2, South Africa 0 Thursday, June 11: Korea Republic 2, Czechia 1 Thursday, June 18: Czechia 1, South Africa 1 Thursday, June 18: Mexico 1 South Korea 0 Wednesday, June 24: Mexico 3, Czechia 0 Wednesday, June 24: South Africa 1, South Korea 0.

news.title: World Cup 2026: A historic halftime show brings music and football together on the biggest stage
news.lede: The 2026 World Cup final was not only remembered for Spain defeating Argentina and claiming its second star. During halftime at MetLife Stadium in New Jersey, FIFA made history by presenting the first halftime show ever held during a World Cup match. The production aimed to bring the biggest soccer event in the world closer to the format of the planet’s most iconic sporting spectacles, with a show designed to unite different generations and musical styles. Under the creative direction of Chris Martin, Coldplay’s lead singer, in collaboration with Global Citizen, the stage welcomed some of the biggest names in international music.

news.title: Patience beats overreliance on Messi: how the final was won and lost
news.lede: Argentina tried to kill the spectacle and make it a physical battle but Spain refused to take the bait and kept passing. A worrying thought as a tepid first half drew to a close: was the half-time show going to be better than the football? Argentina could not match Spain’s possession approach but they had ways to level the playing field. There was physicality from Lionel Scaloni’s side, whose aim was to kill the spectacle before taking control in the second half, and they were allowed to take it to the limit by the Slovenian referee, Slavko Vincic.

news.title: Hits, misses and Muppets: the star-studded World Cup half-time show nobody asked for
news.lede: It zipped along, cramming in Shakira, Madonna and Coldplay, but football fans may not be convinced of need for this mid-match spectacle. Like hydration breaks and Donald Trump’s ability to get refereeing decisions overturned by simply phoning up the president of Fifa and complaining, the final half-time show is an innovation of the 2026 World Cup that comes with a degree of controversy attached. For one thing, its existence automatically means the World Cup final breaks the International Football Association’s 15-minute half-time mandate. For another, its obvious antecedent lies in the NFL’s Super Bowl, where the half-time entertainment has mushroomed over the decades from a few marching bands to one of the big pop events of the year.

news.title: World Cup 2026: What are the extra time and penalty shootout rules?
news.lede: Several games in the 2026 World Cup have already ended in dramatic penalty shootouts.
